summary: Automated hematology analyzers may generate misleading or incomplete data compared with manual microscopy; this discrepancy underscores a gap between reported values from devices and accurate interpretation by clinicians when assessing RBC morphological features such as ring sideroblasts or Heinz bodies. The reliance on automation alone risks missing clinically significant abnormalities necessitating clinician-led review of smears for correct diagnosis.
